decisions.summary: Single-branch hard cut. No backward-compatibility shims, no readRole() migration helper, no soft transition. On upgrade: in-flight agents are killed, state.json files lacking `role` are dropped, config.yaml `overrides` (legacy WorkTypeId map) is silently dropped, defaults seeded for missing workhorses/roles. Users restart any active issues against the new pipeline.

Workhorse Models = exactly 3 user-managed slots (`expensive`, `mid`, `cheap`) at the top of Settings. Every per-role and per-sub-role model picker accepts EITHER a literal model id OR a `workhorse:<slot>` reference. Default config uses workhorse refs everywhere — fresh install fully managed through the 3 dials. Resolution dereferences at spawn time (live cascade, not frozen at picker-click). Unresolved or chained references fail loudly at config-load time with a precise field-pointer error.

Plan and Review default to `workhorse:expensive`. Rationale: planning errors cascade through every downstream bead (Jidoka can catch wrong implementation but not wrong foundation); review is the decision-maker that gates merge. Both deserve the strongest model. Work/Test/Ship default to `workhorse:mid`. Inspect (every-bead self-check) defaults to `workhorse:cheap`; inspect-deep (flagged beads) to `workhorse:mid`.

Drop work phases entirely. The work agent today transitions through exploration → implementation → testing → documentation → review-response with potentially different models per phase. After this PR, work is one undifferentiated mode with one model (workhorse:mid by default) plus two inspect sub-roles. All `state.phase` / `state.workType` reads die. Phase-handoff plumbing is removed.

Reactive Cloister hooks into existing internal event emitters — NOT a new tracker poller. Issue-lifecycle service (src/dashboard/server/services/issue-lifecycle.ts) already emits `issue.*` domain events. Cloister subscribes to those plus existing agent-completion events (agent.completed, work.completed, review.approved, test.passed) and uses stateToRole(IssueState) to spawn the next role. Concurrency guard: skip spawn if an active run for (issueId, role) already exists.

Convoy reviewers (security/correctness/performance/requirements) move from being separate Cloister specialists (with prompt templates at src/lib/cloister/prompts/review/*.prompt-template.md) to Claude Code subagent definitions at .claude/agents/code-review-<flavor>.md, invoked from inside roles/review.md via the Agent tool. Synthesis IS the review role itself — no separate synthesis sub-agent. Each sub-reviewer's model resolves through resolveModel('review', '<flavor>').

Pi harness scope: PAN-1048 ships claude-code-only roles. getAgentRuntimeBaseCommand keeps its existing `pi --mode rpc --model <model>` short-circuit unchanged — Pi has no agent-definition system. A follow-up issue (e.g. PAN-1049) tracks Pi role-body integration. Settings still allows Pi as a per-role harness override; Pi just doesn't read the role's .md body.

Workhorse and per-role/per-sub-role model pickers list models from ALL enabled providers (Anthropic, OpenAI, Google, Z.AI/glm, Kimi, OpenRouter), grouped by provider with provider header rows. Maximum flexibility: workhorses.cheap = kimi-k2.6-flash and workhorses.expensive = glm-4.7-pro are both valid configurations.

Unify `runtime` → `harness` in this PR. The Channels eligibility check (src/lib/agents.ts ~line 775) moves from `state.runtime !== 'claude' && state.runtime !== 'claude-code'` to `state.harness === 'claude-code' && state.role === 'work'` — Channels was always work-agent-only, so the role check makes the intent explicit. M1 carries this; if it gets dropped on the floor, Channels silently breaks for every work agent.

Synthesis IS the review role — there is no separate synthesis sub-agent. The review role reads the diff, fans out 4 parallel convoy sub-runs via the Agent tool, collects their findings, decides approve/request-changes, posts the review comment, and transitions the issue state. Human-merge invariant: review NEVER merges; only approves and transitions.

Test discipline: every refactor bead must keep the existing tests green. If a test references a deleted enum or field, the bead that deletes the enum/field is responsible for updating or replacing the test. No 'test cleanup' bead at the end — tests are part of each bead's acceptance criteria.

Concurrent work runs (PAN-970 swarm parallelism) is out of scope for PAN-1048. The reactive scheduler holds a per-issue concurrency guard (no second run spawned while one is active for the same role on the same issue). The role primitive does not block PAN-970 — it makes it easier to build.

PRD location: this planning session left the PRD at .pan/prd.md (workspace-local). The final bead (z2-move-prd-canonical) moves it to docs/prds/planned/PAN-1048-role-primitive.md so the link in the GitHub issue body works after merge. Do not move it earlier — the PRD is mutated repeatedly during planning.

Deferred X1 legacy .claude/agents/pan-*-agent.md deletion because bd surfaced it before all spawn callsites had migrated to role-based launch. The files are still required by planning and specialist dispatch, so deleting them now would brick active and future agents. Treat X1 as out-of-scope until a follow-up after role spawn migration is verified on main.

Role model foundation uses type-only Role import in config-yaml.ts to avoid a runtime circular dependency with agents.ts. mergeConfigs remains the config-load validation boundary, while derefWorkhorse/resolveModel are exported for role spawn and settings code.

hazards.summary: WorkTypeId has 167 callsites; SpecialistType has 138. The work agent must update every callsite as it migrates through D1 + E2; missed callsites surface as TypeScript errors but the volume is high.
hazards.mitigation: After each enum-deletion bead, run `npm run typecheck` and `npm run lint` and fix every error before moving on. Don't suppress errors with `as any`. Each enum-deletion bead's acceptance criteria includes 'typecheck passes'.

hazards.summary: The convoy reviewer migration (V1 → R3 → S3) changes the spawn mechanism for 4 parallel reviewers. If R3's roles/review.md file does not correctly invoke .claude/agents/code-review-<flavor>.md sub-agents, the review pipeline silently degrades to no-convoy single-reviewer.
hazards.mitigation: S3 includes an end-to-end smoke test (subItem ac5) that validates the full convoy fan-out runs and produces a synthesized decision. R3 has requiresInspection=true so the inspect specialist verifies the file structure before downstream beads land.

hazards.summary: M1 (state.json hard cut) is the data-model break point. If any code path missed in earlier beads still reads state.type/state.agentPhase/state.phase/state.workType/state.complexity/state.handoffCount/state.runtime, the dashboard or spawn paths will break at runtime even though typecheck passes (string-property access on `any`-typed values).
hazards.mitigation: M1 acceptance criterion ac5 explicitly requires `grep returns zero hits in src/` for each deleted field. The work agent must run that grep and resolve every hit before signaling done. This is enforced by the Jidoka inspect for M1 (requiresInspection=true).

hazards.summary: Channels eligibility check at src/lib/agents.ts ~line 775 is easy to miss when deleting `runtime` from AgentState. If Channels silently disables for all work agents, prompt delivery falls back to tmux paste-buffer (not broken, but defeats the experimental Channels feature).
hazards.mitigation: M1 acceptance criterion ac3 explicitly requires moving the Channels gate from state.runtime to `state.harness === 'claude-code' && state.role === 'work'`. The bead description calls this out. Tests in tests/channels-eligibility (or similar) must continue to pass.

hazards.summary: The reactive Cloister scheduler depends on issue-lifecycle events that already exist plus agent-completion events that may not be emitted today. If C1 wires up subscribers for events that nothing fires, transitions break — work completes but review never spawns.
hazards.mitigation: Before C1 implementation, the work agent should grep for each event name (agent.completed, work.completed, review.approved, test.passed) and confirm an emitter exists. If a needed event is missing, C1's scope expands to add the emitter — this is a known acceptable scope creep, called out in C1's narrative. C1 has requiresInspection=true.

hazards.summary: The startup migration in M1 kills in-flight agents whose state.json lacks `role`. If a user has long-running agents at the moment they upgrade, those agents are terminated with no warning. Hard-cut decision (D1) accepted this trade-off.
hazards.mitigation: M1 logs the count of dropped agents at startup. Document the upgrade behavior in the release notes / CLAUDE.md update (z1-update-claude-md-and-docs). Recommend users pause active issues before upgrading.

hazards.summary: Settings UI rewrite (U1-U5) and backend deletion (D1, E1-E5, K1) are loosely coupled — UI assumes config has workhorses/roles fields, backend assumes UI sends new field shapes. If U5 (API settings) lands without the frontend updates, GET /api/settings returns the new shape but the old AgentCardsPanel chokes.
hazards.mitigation: U3 (settings page rewire) blocks U4 (delete old components). UI changes land in a single bead-cluster (U1 → U2,U5 → U3 → U4) with the page rewire as a single atomic point of switch. Manual UAT after U3 lands and again after U4 deletes the old code.

hazards.summary: Untracked files in the workspace (.claude/skills/pan-tts/scripts/tts_daemon.py, .claude/skills/pipeline-status/, three deleted feedback files) are unrelated to PAN-1048. Risk: a bead's `git add` accidentally sweeps them in.
hazards.mitigation: Work agent should never use `git add -A` or `git add .`. Always add specific files by name. The .pan/feedback/* deletions were already on disk at planning start and should be either committed in a separate cleanup or restored — the work agent should not touch them.

hazards.summary: The Cloister machinery deletion in C2 deletes ~30+ functions across specialists.ts, router.ts, deacon.ts, and the prompts/review/ directory. A missed reference to one of those deleted functions (e.g., from a test fixture or an unused-but-imported module) causes typecheck to fail.
hazards.mitigation: C2 acceptance criterion ac8 requires typecheck + lint + tests pass. The bead description lists every function/file to delete. Work agent runs the gates after each subset of deletions, not at the end.

hazards.summary: Contracts migration (E2, K1) crosses the wire-format boundary between server and frontend. If contracts are rebuilt but the frontend isn't recompiled (Vite dev cache, stale dist), runtime errors appear that look like backend bugs.
hazards.mitigation: K1 acceptance criterion ac3 requires `cd packages/contracts && npm run build` followed by a frontend rebuild. Acceptance criterion ac4 requires an end-to-end smoke (open dashboard, observe a running role) before signaling done.

hazards.summary: The bead dependency materialization allowed X1 to become ready too early, despite runtime dependencies on legacy agent definition files.
hazards.mitigation: Do not touch .claude/agents/pan-*-agent.md in PAN-1048 unless the vBRIEF and bd graph are corrected after S2-S5/C1 land. Keep legacy files in place until a separate cleanup issue verifies role-based launch is stable on main.

hazards.summary: The bd dependency graph for PAN-1048 was materialized in reverse, causing terminal cleanup beads to appear before foundation beads.
hazards.mitigation: Corrected the active bd graph enough for f1-role-config-schema to be the sole ready bead before implementing it; continue checking bd ready against the vBRIEF order and fix dependency edges before executing any bead that appears before its prerequisites.

resumePoint.description: Completed workspace-t6t4/f1-role-config-schema. Next: commit this bead, close it, run pan inspect because requiresInspection=true, then continue with bd ready -l pan-1048.

sessionHistory.content: Planning session for PAN-1048 (Unify agent type system: 5 enums → Role primitive). Pre-planning, the user updated the PRD and GitHub issue with the new Workhorse Models concept (3 user-managed slots — expensive/mid/cheap — referenced from per-role and per-sub-role pickers via `workhorse:<slot>` indirection). The user also clarified that plan + review should both default to workhorse:expensive (planning errors cascade; review gates merge). Architectural decisions surfaced via AskUserQuestion: (1) drop work phases entirely; (2) reactive Cloister hooks into existing internal event emitters rather than building a new tracker poller; (3) hard cut on upgrade — kill in-flight agents, drop legacy fields, no readRole() shim; (4) workhorse pickers list all enabled providers grouped; (5) Pi parity deferred to follow-up; (6) convoy prompt templates migrate to .claude/agents/code-review-<flavor>.md invoked via the Agent tool; (7) runtime → harness unification in this PR. Codebase mapping confirmed: 138 SpecialistType callsites, 167 WorkTypeId callsites, no `roles/` directory exists yet, IssueLifecycle service already emits issue.* domain events, convoy reviewers today are separate Cloister specialists (not subagents). Decomposed into 32 beads with 57 dependency edges across 7 phases (Foundation, Convoy migration, Role files, Per-role spawn wiring, Reactive Cloister + machinery deletion, Settings UI, Backend dead-code deletion + enum deletions + contracts + state.json hard cut + cleanup).
sessionHistory.note: Planning complete. 32 beads written to .pan/spec.vbrief.json. Continue.json populated with 13 decisions and 10 hazards.
Moved .pan/prd.md to docs/prds/planned/PAN-1048-role-primitive.md, marked z2-move-prd-canonical acceptance criteria complete, and left .pan/prd.md absent from the working tree.

Deferred workspace-bnrg/X1 after user correction: legacy pan agent definition files are still required by current planning and specialist spawn paths. Reverted accidental code-comment edits and preserved only the vBRIEF deferral metadata.

Implemented Role type plus workhorse/role model config schema, derefWorkhorse, resolveModel, config-load validation through mergeConfigs, and vitest coverage for role resolution, sub-role override, missing-slot and chained-ref errors. Typecheck and targeted role config tests pass.
